Job.JobDescription:
Robichaud High School is looking for a Basketball Athletics/Activities/ to join our program. Apply now.
Westwood Athletics Position: Varsity Boys Basketball Coach Start Date: 2026-2027 School Year Winter Season Reports to: Athletic Director and Principal Work Hours : Hours & Calendar Will Reflect High School Athletics Basketball Schedule Education/Training/Experience : Must be available to perform coaching responsibilities, games and practices outside of your contractual day. (Daily contractual hours will not be modified to accommodate coaching schedule.) Basketball knowledge and experience. Basketball coaching experience, preferred. Understanding of Michigan High School Athletic Association rules and regulations. Ability to communicate and work well with High School students. Ability to communicate and work well with parents, staff and the community. Michigan Certified Teacher, preferred. Must be a High School Graduate or equivalent. Responsibilities: Coach's individual participants in the skills necessary for excellent achievement in the sport involved. Plans and schedules a regular program of practice in season. Works closely with other coaches and/or principals in scheduling intramural and interscholastic contests. Recommends purchase of equipment, supplies, and uniforms as appropriate. Maintains necessary attendance forms, insurance records, evidence of physical examination and similar paperwork. Oversee the safety conditions of the facility or area in which the assigned sport is conducted at all times those students are present. Aids in establishment of performance criteria for interscholastic competition in his sport. Enforces disciplined and sportsmanlike behavior at all times and establishes and oversees penalties for breach of such standards by individual students. Participates in annual MHSAA Sponsored rules interpretation meeting for their individual sport(s). To abide by MHSAA by-laws, at least one coach form each sport must attend rules interpretation meetings. Attend additional meetings when specifically requested by the athletic director and/or principal. Bring recommendations for volunteers and assistants to the principal and Board of Education for approval. Work collaboratively with other coaches to provide students with opportunities for physical conditioning Outside of the season, including summer conditioning and workouts. Work collaboratively with administration to support the program while at the same time controlling district expenses Is responsible for student supervision and safety before and after all activities and events both home and away. Maintains consistent communication with Athletic Director Develop positive self-image with athletics Attends clinics / workshops related to sport. Utilize sound teaching methods to develop skills / techniques. Demonstrate positive team sportsmanship toward opponents and officials. Demonstrates effective management of pre-contest details. Other duties as assigned by the Athletic Director. This posting should not be construed to imply that these requirements are the exclusive standards of the position or that this is the exclusive list of job functions or expectations associated with this position. The requirements listed above are representative of the knowledge, skill and/or ability required for this position. LANGUAGE SKILLS: Ability to read and interpret documents such as safety rules, operating and maintenance instructions, and procedure manuals. Ability to write routine reports and correspondence. Ability to speak effectively before groups of customers or employees of organization. MATHEMATICAL SKILLS : Add, subtract, and multiply and divide in all units of measure, using whole numbers, common fractions, and decimals. REASONING ABILITY: Apply common sense understanding to carry out instructions furnished in written, oral, or diagram form. Deal with problems involving a few concrete variables in standardized situations. OTHER SKILLS and ABILITIES: Work with parents and children of diverse cultural backgrounds. Develop effective working relationships with students, staff, and the school community. Perform duties with awareness of all district requirements and Board of Education policies. Physical Demands: The physical demands described here are representative of those that must be met by an employee to successfully perform the essential functions of this job. Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential job functions: The employee must occasionally lift and move up to 25 pounds in supplies, which requires bending, stooping and lifting. The employee must be able to use a variety of instructional materials in the classroom. The employee must use hands and arms to manipulate objects. The employee must use keyboards, tools and other controls. The employee must sit and stand for long periods of time. The employee must have normal vision and hearing with or without aid. The employee must be able to move about the assigned location unaided during the day. Work Environment: The work environment characteristics described here are representative of those an employee encounters while performing the essential functions of this job: The noise level in the work environment will vary and can range from soft to loud. Often the noise level in the work environment will be moderate to loud. The employee is required to interact with students, staff, parents/guardians and occasionally with the public. At times, the employee may be directly responsible for the safety and well-being of students. How to Apply: Apply online at: https://www.westwoodschools.net/apps/pages/ and click the "Job Opportunities" tab. SALARY: Per negotiated agreement, paid through EduStaff. Non-Discrimination Statement: The Board of Education does not discriminate on the basis of race, color, national origin, sex, (including sexual orientation and transgender identity) disability, age, religion, height, weight, marital or family status, military status, ancestry, genetic information or any other legally protected category, (collectively, "protected Classes"), in its programs and activities, including employment opportunities.

Job.JobTitle: Head Varsity Track and Field Coach
Job.JobDescription:
Romulus High School is looking for a Track & Field Athletics/Activities/ to join our program. Apply now.
POSITION TITLE: Head Varsity Track & Field Coach DEPARTMENT: Romulus High School REPORTS TO: Director of Athletics PAY SCHEDULE: As per REA Collective Bargaining Agreement and experience EFFECTIVE: Immediate Qualifications : Experience with School High School Track & Field Programing. Must be available to perform coaching responsibilities, games and practices outside of your contractual day. (Daily contractual hours will not be modified to accommodate coaching schedule.) Track & Field coaching experience preferred. Track & Field knowledge and playing experience. Understanding of Michigan High School Athletic Association rules and regulations. Ability to communicate and work well with high school students. Ability to communicate and work well with parents, Booster Club and the community. Michigan Certified Teacher, preferred. Responsibilities : Counsel students / athletes about scholarships and the importance of grades. Expose students/athletes to surrounding colleges. Monitor grades of students / athletes in the Track & Field program. Maintain academic eligibility log for student athlete participation. Assist with the implementation of academic intervention strategies for student athletes to maintain academic performance and eligibility standards. Adhere to academic eligibility for student athlete participation in games, conferences and tournaments. Meet with parents of Team members, prior to the start of season. Builds strong relationships with parents and the school community to develop a culture. for academic accomplishment aligned with athletic achievement. Responsible for the preparation and submittal of a yearly budget and bids to order necessary equipment. Assist track team in making decisions, planning events and implementing plans. Coordinate with the Track Coaching Staff, a practice schedule and adhere to the practice schedule for the entire season. Teach and coach student athletes in the techniques and skills needed to ensure a successful track program. Be a positive role model and motivator. Encourage student athletes to be student role models in the building; assist building in establishing Positive Behavior and Intervention Support programming. Attend all meetings as required by the MHSAA and WWAC League. Excellent time management, communication and organizational skills. Ability to interact positively with students, staff and parents. Skilled in collaborating with other professionals. Comply with district policies and procedures (i.e., academic eligibility of student athletes, accident reporting, cash handling, travel and transportation requests, parent / assistant / volunteer approval and authorization procedures, etc.). Ability to work with minimal supervision. Other duties, within the athletic department, as they relate to the sport. This posting should not be construed to imply that these requirements are the exclusive standards of the position or that this is the exclusive list of job functions or expectations associated with this position. The requirements listed above are representative of the knowledge, skill and/or ability required for this position. Physical Demands : The physical demands described here are representative of those that must be met by an employee to successfully perform the essential functions of this job. Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential job functions: The employee must occasionally stoop, bend, kneel, crouch, reach and twist while performing their job duties. The employee must be able to lift and move up to 25 pounds in supplies which requires bending, stooping, lifting, and reaching. The employee may have to push and/or pull light to moderate amounts of weight. The employee must be able to use and operate a variety of office equipment requiring repetitive hand movement and fine coordination including the use of a computer keyboard. The employee must use hands and arms to manipulate objects. The employee will have extensive verbal communication with administrators, staff, students, parents, & guests to communicate, to receive, and exchange information. The employee must use keyboards, tools, and other controls. The employee must sit and stand for prolonged periods of time. The employee must have normal vision and hearing with or without aid. The employee must be able to move about the assigned work location during the day. Work Environment : The work environment characteristics described here are representative of those an employee encounters while performing the essential functions of this job: The noise level in the work environment will vary and can range from soft to loud. Often the noise level is the work environment will be soft to moderate. The employee is required to interact with students, staff, parents / guardians and occasionally with the public. At times, the employee may be directly responsible for the safety and well-being of students. Application Procedure : Internal: Email a letter of interest to Jaresha Obey at jmobey@romulusk12.org . External: Apply online at www.romulusk12.org or through Wayne RESA Applitrack Closing: Until Filled

Job.JobDescription:
Summit Academy North High School is looking for a Volleyball Athletics/Activities/ to join our program. Apply now.
Summit Academy North is currently seeking a positive, creative and energetic Substitute Varsity Volleyball Coach to join our Summit Family! Do you have a passion for creativity and innovation within the classroom? Are you interested in working with a diverse population of students in a safe, family friendly environment? If so, Summit Academy North is the place for you! Our doors at Summit Academy North have been open for 25 years, educating students from Preschool through 12th grade. Located in Romulus, MI, our beautiful buildings are filled with over 1,600 inspiring students. On any day, one can expect to find classrooms bursting with energy, as well as shared leadership, collaboration, and the freedom of our teachers to educate students to their fullest potential. We are recognized for our powerful mentoring program and year round professional development practices. We offer before and after school activities for all students, including band, music and an athletic program. Inspiring staff can be found every day throughout the buildings at Summit Academy North, helping students reach new heights in education. Could that staff member be you? JOB SUMMARY: The Coach is responsible to organize and conduct the practice sessions, prepare the team to participate in competitive events, care for all entrusted materials and supplies, lead the participants by example, follow the established guidelines of the district, and generally implement the other responsibilities of the position in the best interest of the students and school district congruently with the Varsity Head Coach. DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES: 1. Provide appropriate supervision at all times for all individuals in the program and provide an updated list of all individuals participating in the program to the Athletic Director. 2. Provide a schedule of practices, meetings, and similar events, considered an integral part of the activity and indicate what specific objectives you intend to accomplish. 3. Meet with the Athletic Director and Head Coach to review the program, including activities, meeting times, and places at the onset of the program. Clarify any problems or questions that arise. 4. Provide a pre- and post-inventory of all materials, equipment, and supplies connected with the activity to the Athletic Director. 5. Request, through official district procedures, facilities necessary to conduct the activity under your direction, i.e., rooms, fields, buses. 6. Request, through official district procedures, any materials or equipment required in the activity. Receive prior approval before purchases are made or material/equipment ordered. 7. Promote scholarship among participants and enforce any rules of eligibility that exist. 8. Recognize the interrelationship between the educational program and extracurricular activities, being careful not to promote the activity to the detriment of the educational program or the students. 9. Utilize acceptable techniques in teaching students participating in the activity and promote good sportsmanship/citizenship. 10. Maintain a fair, unprejudiced relationship with all activity participants and set a proper example for all participants to follow. 11. Monitor the physical condition of participants; insist that physicals, if required, are completed prior to participation and that appropriate medical attention if required during the course of the activity is not delayed or ignored. 12. Discharge all responsibilities of the activity in a complete and satisfactory manner prior to requesting payment for services. 13. Promote and participate actively in any booster groups, which support the activity. 14. Assist in or handle directly the procurement of judges, chaperones, officials, and opponents required of the activity. 15. Refrain from drinking, gambling, using profanity, abusive language, physical force or threats with the participants and take appropriate steps in line with district policies if students become involved in the aforementioned activities. 16. Submit a brief written report at the conclusion of the activity if requested by the administrator. 17. Assume other duties relating to the activity as assigned by the administrator, in line with Board policy, and administrative regulations. QUALIFICATIONS: Some college playing experience is preferred. Coaching experience preferred. Valid teaching certificate that meets NCA requirements preferred. Ability to maintain an effective working relationship with booster club. Must possess good written and oral communication skills and possess the ability to interact effectively with a broad spectrum of individuals and organizations. High energy level Must possess good judgment and effective decision making skills. CPR, first aid certification and training Strength training experience and education Such alternatives to the above qualifications the Summit Academy may find appropriate and acceptable. APPLICATION PROCEDURE: The applicant should provide the following to Keith Stephens via email at kstephens@summitacademy.com by April 27th, 2026: Letter of application detailing all experience appropriate to the position. Resume which includes: Letters of recommendation To learn more about Summit Academy Schools, please visit our website at www.summitacademy.com CS Partners/Partner Solutions provides equal employment opportunities to all employees and applicants for employment and prohibits unlawful discrimination and harassment of any type without regard to race, color, religion, age, sex, national origin, disability, genetic information, military or veteran status, sexual orientation and gender identity or expression, marital status, familial status, height, weight, or any other protected status under federal, state or local laws.

Job.JobDescription:
Wayne-Westland Community School D. is looking for a Swimming and Diving Athletics/Activities/ to join our program. Apply now.
April 15, 2026 POSITION: Varsity Girls Swimming Head Coach JOB SUMMARY: This position is responsible for coaching and providing direction to the student athletes so that they might achieve a high level of skill, as well as an appreciation for discipline, sportsmanship and teamwork in Swimming. This position contributes to the educational program as a whole and the growth of the students. MINIMAL QUALIFICATIONS: Bachelor Degree preferred. Previous varsity head coaching experience is preferred. 3 years beyond high school graduation. CPR/First Aid Successful completion of all required online health/safety courses. Previous Coaching experience preferred. Experience working in a school setting and with others from diverse backgrounds. Knowledge and understanding pertaining to sport, strategies, rules etc. Understanding of Michigan High School Athletic Association rules and regulations. Meet the guidelines set by the Woodhaven Brownstown School District. (Includes: drug screening/criminal background checks). MAJOR DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES (May include but not be limited to): Follows and maintains knowledge of all District policies and procedures. Instructs and teaches skill sets and techniques necessary for individual and team achievement. Plans and sets-up activities, practices and games. Ensures a district approved coach is present at all practices and games. Develops appropriate training programs and practice schedules. Ensures proper cleaning, storage and maintenance of all athletic equipment, uniforms and updated inventories are prepared in collaboration with Head Coach. Ensures appropriate behavior of student athletes including locker room, bus, practices, etc. Follows school rules and Woodhaven Brownstown School District regulations regarding conduct and eligibility of athletic activities. Maintains a safe environment and facilities for student athletes in accordance with Athletic Department Guidance. Follow all MHSAA rules and Guidelines, including appropriate certification for coaches. Reports rosters to the Athletic Director in a timely manner. Provides a proper end of the year report including needed items as deemed by the Athletic Director. Professionally represents the school and the district in interactions with students, parents, community, staff and the media.

Job.JobDescription:
Crestwood High School is looking for a Golf Athletics/Activities/ to join our program. Apply now.
Crestwood School District Athletic Department Girls Varsity Golf Coach (fall season) If hired, the coach will perform his/her duties and responsibilities under the direct supervision of the Athletic Director, in compliance with the policies established by the Crestwood Board of Education and the MHSAA. STEP ONE : Interested persons should submit a letter of interest and resume with references and two letters of recommendation. Posting will remain open until filled. Information should be forwarded to: : Crestwood High School Attn: Jeff Hachigian, Athletic Director 1501 N. Beech Daly Dearborn Heights, MI 48127 Email: jhachigian@csdmi.org In addition to the Step One requirement (above), STEP TWO : Interested applicants must complete an online application on Crestwood's District website (www.csdm.k12.mi.us). Click Employment Opportunity, then fill out an application. Qualifications Required: - Golf coaching experience - Knowledge of coaching and training techniques - Basic knowledge of first aid - Good character with the ability to serve as a positive role model to student-athletes - Willingness to work cooperatively in all aspects of the interscholastic athletic program

LINCOLN PARK PUBLIC SCHOOLS 1650 Champaign Lincoln Park, Michigan 48146 ANNOUNCEMENT OF VACANCY 2026 -2027 School Year Fall Season POSITION: High School Girls Swim Coach QUALIFICATIONS: High School Diploma Proven ability to work with students in a non-academic setting Successful coaching experience in the sport preferred Recognize the importance of making a commitment to work with the rest of the coaching staff for this sport and the Athletic Director Demonstrate the ability to work with students, parents, community members, fellow coaches, and game officials on a positive and professional basis Proven positive role model regarding dress, appropriate use of language, appropriate physical contact, and appropriate mental/psychological approach to working with students, parents, community members, and game officials Other duties as assigned by the Athletic Director or the Building Principal Such alternatives to the above qualifications as the Board of Education and/or Superintendent may find appropriate and acceptable. Job.JobDescription:
Wayne-Westland Community School D. is looking for a Flag Football Athletics/Activities/ to join our program. Apply now.
Position Title: Boys Flag Football Coach Reports To: Athletic Director / School Principal Schedule: April 23, 2026  May 23, 2026 Employment Type: Seasonal / Stipend Position/ 1 Year Contract Position Summary The Boys Flag Football Coach is responsible for developing, organizing, and leading the Boys Flag Football program and oversight of all assistant coaches on staff. This position provides direction to student-athletes to help them achieve excellence both on and off the field, while fostering an appreciation for discipline, sportsmanship, teamwork, and academic achievement. The coach will collaborate closely with school leadership, athletic staff, and families to ensure the success and growth of the Flag Football program in alignment with HPS's academic and athletic standards. Duties and Responsibilities Plan, organize, and direct all aspects of the Flag Football programs, including practices, games and conditioning. Develop and implement game strategies, practice plans, and skill development sessions to enhance team performance. Supervise and mentor assistant coaches, ensuring alignment with the program's philosophy and expectations. Provide leadership and support to the coaching staff to ensure program continuity and player development. Promote good sportsmanship, positive team culture, and academic accountability among student-athletes. Communicate regularly with the Athletic Director, School Operations Manager, and Principal regarding team matters, student-athlete welfare, and scheduling. Represent HPS Athletics positively within the community and at all interscholastic events. Education & Experience Associate's degree preferred; Bachelor's degree preferred. Previous coaching experience required. Proven ability to teach Flag Football fundamentals, sportsmanship, leadership, and discipline. Experience working with students in an educational setting . Knowledge, Skills & Abilities Strong understanding of Flag Football systems, player development, and conditioning methods. Excellent organizational, interpersonal, and communication skills. Ability to lead by example, motivate young athletes, and foster a positive team environment. Ability to work collaboratively with teachers, administrators, parents, and the broader community. Proficient in Microsoft Word, Excel, PowerPoint, and general computer operations. Strong problem-solving and conflict-resolution skills. Commitment to HPS's mission of academic and athletic excellence. Work Schedule & Availability Must be available after school for practices, games, and team activities. SALARY: Per the HFT Teacher Contract Hamtramck Public Schools is an equal opportunity employer committed to fostering an inclusive environment for all staff, coaches, and student-athletes.
Job.CompanyDescription: Wayne-Westland Community School D. coaching jobs

Job.JobTitle: Girls Varsity Flag Football Coach Spring 2026
Job.JobDescription:
Wayne-Westland Community School D. is looking for a Flag Football Athletics/Activities/ to join our program. Apply now.
Position Title: Girls Varsity Flag Football Coach Reports To: Athletic Director / School Principal Schedule: April 24, 2026 – June 5, 2026 Employment Type: Seasonal / Stipend Position/ 1 Year Contract Position Summary The Girls Varsity Flag Football Coach is responsible for developing, organizing, and leading the Varsity Girls Flag Football program and oversight of all assistant coaches on staff. This position provides direction to student-athletes to help them achieve excellence both on and off the field, while fostering an appreciation for discipline, sportsmanship, teamwork, and academic achievement. The coach will collaborate closely with school leadership, athletic staff, and families to ensure the success and growth of the Flag Football program in alignment with HPS’s academic and athletic standards. Duties and Responsibilities Plan, organize, and direct all aspects of the Flag Football programs, including practices, games and conditioning. Develop and implement game strategies, practice plans, and skill development sessions to enhance team performance. Supervise and mentor assistant coaches, ensuring alignment with the program’s philosophy and expectations. Provide leadership and support to the coaching staff to ensure program continuity and player development. Promote good sportsmanship, positive team culture, and academic accountability among student-athletes. Communicate regularly with the Athletic Director, School Operations Manager, and Principal regarding team matters, student-athlete welfare, and scheduling. Represent HPS Athletics positively within the community and at all interscholastic events. Education & Experience Associate’s degree preferred; Bachelor’s degree preferred. Previous coaching experience required. Proven ability to teach Flag Football fundamentals, sportsmanship, leadership, and discipline. Experience working with students in an educational setting . Knowledge, Skills & Abilities Strong understanding of Flag Football systems, player development, and conditioning methods. Excellent organizational, interpersonal, and communication skills. Ability to lead by example, motivate young athletes, and foster a positive team environment. Ability to work collaboratively with teachers, administrators, parents, and the broader community. Proficient in Microsoft Word, Excel, PowerPoint, and general computer operations. Strong problem-solving and conflict-resolution skills. Commitment to HPS’s mission of academic and athletic excellence. Work Schedule & Availability Must be available after school for practices, games, and team activities. SALARY: Per the HFT Teacher Contract Hamtramck Public Schools is an equal opportunity employer committed to fostering an inclusive environment for all staff, coaches, and student-athletes.

Job.JobDescription:
Flat Rock Community High School is looking for a Bowling Athletics/Activities/ to join our program. Apply now.
Posting Date: Friday, April 10, 2026 Position: Varsity Boys Bowling Head Coach Qualifications: Must be a high school graduate; college graduate preferred. Valid certifications (or willingness to obtain) including: CPR/AED and First Aid MHSAA CAP (any level) Previous bowling participation required; coaching experience preferred. Knowledge and understanding pertaining to sport, strategies, rules, etc. Knowledge and Understanding of Michigan High School Athletic Association (MHSAA) rules and regulations. Demonstrated ability to work effectively with high school student-athletes, parents, and staff. Demonstrated coaching strategies and methods that align with the District's Student-Focused Coaching and Leadership Philosophies. Duties & Responsibilities: Manage and supervise athletic activities, contests, and practice sessions for the team you are assigned to, in accordance with the FRCS Athletic Coaching Handbook and District Policies. Maintain a consistent presence and commitment to the program, prioritizing responsibilities to the program throughout the season. Communicate and enforce all school, league, and Michigan High School Athletic Association (MHSAA) rules and regulations. Promote academic achievement, sportsmanship, and personal development among team members. Work collaboratively with the athletic department and coaching staff to ensure a safe and well-organized program. Represent Flat Rock Community Schools in a professional and respectful manner at all practices, meets, and school-related events. Other duties as assigned by the Athletic Director Rate of Pay: Per Schedule "B" of the FREA Collective Bargaining Agreement Posting Deadline: Until Filled

All candidates need to submit an on line application which includes a cover letter, resume, and letters of reference. ON LINE APPLICATIONS ONLY Qualified candidates must be a high school graduate with three years of coaching experience preferred. The candidate should have experience as a player and or coach in the sport of volleyball, with an up to date certification in first aid and CPR prior to employment. Documentation must be on file with nursing services prior to the start of work. The coach needs to be able to promote an atmosphere that is consistent with educational athletics.

Job.JobDescription:
CANTON HIGH SCHOOL is looking for a Cross Country Athletics/Activities/ to join our program. Apply now.
POSITION : Boys Cross Country  Assistant Coach Canton High School MINIMUM QUALIFICATIONS : 1. High school graduate or equivalent. 2. Applicants must have experience in high school level coaching or playing at college or university level in sport to which they are applying. 3. Applicant must have a time schedule compatible with that of the high school. 4. CPR and/or first aide training preferred. 5. Demonstrated ability to work with parents and children. REPORTS TO : Assistant Principal of Activities and Athletics RATE OF PAY : According to the Master Agreement - Schedule "B" BEGINNING DATE : Fall 2026 DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES : 1. The assistant coach will be responsible for the entire organization of the sport to which he/she is assigned. 2. The assistant coach will be directly responsible for all equipment in his/her sport. A complete inventory of all equipment will be filed with the director at the end of each season. 3. The assistant coach will be responsible for all awards presented in his/her sport and a season summary. 4. Bending, lifting, and overhead work required. 5. Performs other tasks as assigned. This description is intended to describe the essential job functions, the general supplemental functions and the essential requirements for the performance of this job. It is not an exhaustive list of all duties, responsibilities and requirements of a person so classified. Other functions may be assigned and management retains the right to add or change the duties at any time. EVALUATION : Performance of this position will be evaluated annually. METHOD OF APPLICATION: Internal and external applicants should complete the online application in the Frontline system. External applicants will be hired and paid by EDUstaff. www.EDUstaff.org DEADLINE FOR APPLICATION: Open until filled Tiffany Roberts Assistant Principal of Activities and Athletics Canton High School 8415 Canton Center Road Canton, MI 48187 POSTED: 03/09/26

REPORTS TO : Assistant Principal of Activities and Athletics RATE OF PAY : According to the P-CEA Master Agreement - Schedule "B" BEGINNING DATE : Spring 2026 DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES : 1. The head coach will be responsible for the entire organization of the sport to which he/she is assigned. 2. The head coach will be directly responsible for all equipment in his/her sport. A complete inventory of all equipment will be filed with the director at the end of each season. 3. The head coach will be responsible for all awards presented in his/her sport and a season summary. 4. Bending, lifting, and overhead work required. 5. Performs other tasks as assigned. This description is intended to describe the essential job functions, the general supplemental functions and the essential requirements for the performance of this job. It is not an exhaustive list of all duties, responsibilities and requirements of a person so classified. Other functions may be assigned and management retains the right to add or change the duties at any time. EVALUATION : Performance of this position will be evaluated annually. METHOD OF APPLICATION : Internal and external applicants should complete the online application in the Frontline system. External applicants will be hired and paid by EDUstaff. www.EDUstaff.org DEADLINE FOR APPLICATION: Open until filled Chechaka Johnson Assistant Principal of Activities and Athletics Plymouth High School 8400 N. Beck Canton, MI 48187 POSTED: 12/05/25

Job.JobTitle: Southfield A and T - Appendix B - Football Assistant Coach 2026-2027 (EXTERNAL)
Job.JobDescription:
Southfield A&T High School Start Date/End Date: August is looking for a Football Athletics/Activities/ to join our program. Apply now.
Position Title: Assistant Football Coach Reports To: Building Principal/Athletic Director Start Date/End Date: August 2026-October 2026 (2026-2027 School Year - FALL ATHLETICS) Salary: $2,700.00 Stipend per the SEA CBA (paid at the end of the season based on the payment calendar) Position Summary: The Assistant Football Coach is responsible for assisting with all aspects of the high school football program. This includes coaching student-athletes, developing game strategies, organizing practices, ensuring academic accountability, and fostering leadership, discipline, and sportsmanship. The coach must demonstrate a strong commitment to student development and uphold the school's values both on and off the field. Key Responsibilities: Develop and implement practice plans, game strategies, and conditioning programs. Teach fundamental football techniques and advanced skills to student athletes. Promote academic success and character development among team members. Supervise assistant coaches and delegate responsibilities as appropriate. Monitor and maintain inventory of equipment and uniforms. Collaborate with the Athletic Director on scheduling games, travel, and budget needs. Ensure compliance with school, district, league, and state athletic regulations. Maintain open communication with players, parents, school staff, and the community. Foster a safe, respectful, and inclusive environment for all participants. Serve as a positive role model and mentor for student-athletes. Organize and participate in team fundraising, community outreach, and promotional events. Evaluate player performance and provide regular feedback and support for improvement. Enforce discipline and team rules consistently and fairly. Oversee offseason conditioning and training programs. Qualifications: Prior coaching experience at the high school level or higher (head coaching experience preferred). In-depth knowledge of football rules, strategies, and safety practices. Strong leadership, organizational, and communication skills. Ability to demonstrate skills and drills. Ability to motivate and mentor student athletes. Bachelor's degree in education, Sports Management, or related field preferred.

Job.JobTitle: Southfield A and T - Appendix B - Soccer Head Coach (Boys) 2026-2027 (EXTERNAL)
Job.JobDescription:
Southfield A&T High School Start Date/End Date: August is looking for a Soccer Athletics/Activities/ to join our program. Apply now.
Position Title: Soccer Coach Reports To: Building Principal/Athletic Director Start Date/End Date: August 2026 -October 2026 (2026-2027 School Year - FALL ATHLETICS) Salary: $4,200.00 Stipend per the SEA CBA (paid at the end of the season based on the payment calendar) Job Summary: The Soccer Coach is responsible for leading, developing, and managing the soccer program while fostering a positive and competitive environment. This includes planning practices, coaching games, mentoring student-athletes, and promoting sportsmanship, discipline, and teamwork. The coach will work collaboratively with school staff, parents, and athletic department leadership to ensure the program's success on and off the field. Key Responsibilities: Plan, organize, and run effective soccer practices and training sessions. Teach fundamental and advanced soccer techniques, strategies, and game rules. Prepare the team for competitions and manage all aspects of game-day coaching. Promote academic success, character development, and personal growth among players. Ensure the safety and well-being of all athletes during practices, games, and travel. Maintain equipment inventory and ensure proper use and storage. Coordinate schedules with the athletic department and manage logistics for practices and games. Communicate regularly with athletes, parents, and school administration. Promote a culture of sportsmanship, discipline, accountability, and respect. Attend league meetings, professional development opportunities, and obtain required certifications. Follow and enforce all school, district, and athletic department policies and procedures. Qualifications: Prior coaching experience in soccer at the high school level, club level or higher. Complete understanding of the game, including rules, strategies, and player development. Ability to demonstrate skills and drills. Strong leadership, communication, and organizational skills. Ability to work with diverse groups of student-athletes and staff. CPR/First Aid certification (or willingness to obtain).

Job.JobTitle: Southfield A and T - Appendix B - Golf Coach (Girls) 2026-2027 (EXTERNAL)
Job.JobDescription:
Southfield A&T High School Start Date/End Date: August is looking for a Golf Athletics/Activities/ to join our program. Apply now.
Position Title: Golf Coach Girls Reports To: Building Principal/Athletic Director Start Date/End Date: August 2026-October 2026 (2026-2027 School Year - FALL ATHLETICS) Salary: $3,600.00 Stipend per the SEA CBA (paid at the end of the season based on the payment calendar) Position Summary: The Head Golf Coach is responsible for leading, organizing, and managing the high school golf program. This role includes teaching golf fundamentals, promoting teamwork and sportsmanship, ensuring student-athletes meet academic and behavioral standards, and managing the logistics of matches and practices. The coach is expected to foster a positive environment where athletes can develop both their golf skills and character. Key Responsibilities: Plan and conduct practice sessions that focus on skill development, game strategy, and physical conditioning. Instruct student-athletes on the rules, techniques, and etiquette of golf. Organize and supervise team practices, matches, and tournaments. Promote academic achievement and personal growth among players. Coordinate with the Athletic Director regarding scheduling, transportation, and budgetary needs. Monitor and maintain all golf equipment, uniforms, and team supplies. Maintain eligibility records and enforce team, school, and district rules. Foster a culture of integrity, accountability, and sportsmanship. Communicate regularly with student-athletes, parents, staff, and school administration. Oversee offseason development and encourage participation in golf-related activities and events. Serve as a positive role model and mentor to student-athletes. Qualifications: Prior experience playing and coaching golf at the high school level but preferably on the collegiate level or higher. Knowledge of golf techniques, rules, and course management. Strong organizational and leadership skills. Ability to teach and inspire student-athletes with varying levels of ability. Must be flexible for tournament schedules.

This includes teaching cheers, stunts, and dance routines; promoting school spirit; always ensuring safety; and fostering teamwork, discipline, and leadership among team members. The coach will work closely with the athletic department, school administration, and parents to support athletic events, competitions, and school functions. Key Responsibilities: Plan and conduct practices focused on cheer techniques, stunting, tumbling, and performance. Lead the cheer team in promoting school spirit at games, pep rallies, and special events. Choreograph routines for halftime shows, assemblies, and competitions. Always emphasize safety, particularly during stunts and tumbling activities. Promote positive team culture, including sportsmanship, responsibility, and leadership. Ensure all participants meet eligibility, academic, and attendance requirements. Manage uniforms, equipment, and team fundraising efforts. Maintain open and professional communication with student-athletes, parents, and school staff. Supervise athletes during all practices, games, competitions, and travel (if applicable). Support and enforce all school, district, and league policies. Qualifications: Prior experience as a head cheer coach at the high school or club level or higher required. Knowledge of cheer techniques, stunts, and performance skills. Ability to demonstrate skills and drills and routines. Strong leadership, organization, and communication skills. Must be able to supervise and motivate students in a positive manner and fully experienced in MHSAA guidelines. Preferred Qualifications: Experience coaching high school, or competitive level or higher. Experience with music editing and choreography.

Job.JobDescription:
Southfield A&T High School Start Date/End Date: August is looking for a Volleyball Athletics/Activities/ to join our program. Apply now.
Position Title: Volleyball Head Coach Reports To: Building Principal/Athletic Director Start Date/End Date: August 2026-October 2026 (2026-2027 School Year - FALL ATHLETICS) Salary: $4,200.00 Stipend per the SEA CBA (paid at the end of the season based on the payment calendar) Position Summary: The Volleyball Coach is responsible for developing and leading a competitive volleyball program that emphasizes skill development, teamwork, sportsmanship, and academic achievement. The coach will plan, organize, and supervise all aspects of the volleyball program while fostering a positive environment that supports the overall mission of the school or organization. Key Responsibilities: Plan and conduct effective practices focused on skill development, game strategy, conditioning, and teamwork. Provide positive leadership and mentorship to student-athletes both on and off the court. Teach the fundamentals of volleyball including serving, passing, setting, hitting, blocking, and defense. Develop game plans, analyze opponent strategies, and make real-time decisions during matches. Promote a culture of high standards, accountability, and respect among players. Maintain a safe and inclusive environment for all athletes. Collaborate with the Athletic Director to coordinate scheduling of games, transportation, and facilities. Ensure players meet academic and eligibility requirements and uphold conduct expectations. Communicate effectively with parents, players, school staff, and athletic department. Manage equipment and uniforms, including inventory and maintenance. Recruit and retain student-athletes to build a sustainable and competitive program. Comply with all school, league, and state athletic association rules and regulations. Qualifications: Prior experience coaching volleyball at the high school, collegiate, or club level. Knowledge of volleyball techniques, rules, and strategies. Strong leadership, communication, and organizational skills. Ability to demonstrate skills and drills. Ability to motivate and inspire student-athletes. CPR/First Aid Certification (or willingness to obtain). Ability to work evenings and weekends as required. Preferred Attributes: Collegiate playing experience or advanced volleyball training background. Proven track record of developing successful volleyball teams or programs. Demonstrated ability to build positive relationships with students, staff, and the community.

Job.JobDescription:
West Bloomfield High School is looking for a Tennis Athletics/Activities/ to join our program. Apply now.
REQUIREMENTS : Experience in the sport as a participant Past coaching experience First-aid and sports medicine training preferred Punctuality and good attendance Teaching certification and experience preferred PERFORMANCE RESPONSIBILITIES : Organize and/or participate in a summer league and team camp Available to conduct fundamental skill clinics for elementary and middle school students Promote academics as a major priority Communicate positively with athletes, coaching staff, parents, administrators, and community Show an expressed interest in the welfare of the total athletic program as well as the other areas of the district's educational program Observe and administer all school and district policies Organize and inventory equipment Submit all administrative material by deadlines Plan and schedule a regular program of practice in and out of season Work closely with the Athletic Director in scheduling interscholastic contests Recommend purchase of equipment, supplies, and uniforms, as appropriate Maintain necessary attendance forms, insurance records, and similar paperwork Oversee the safety conditions of the facility or area in which assigned sport is conducted at all times that students are present, and make recommendations to the Athletic Director Establish performance criteria for eligibility in interscholastic competition in this sport Enforce discipline and sportsmanlike behavior at all times, and establish and oversee penalties for breach of such standards by individual students Such other duties as may be assigned SALARY : In accordance with the current W.B.E.A. Job.JobTitle: Varsity Sideline and Competitive Cheer Coach
Job.JobDescription:
Avondale High School is looking for a Dance Team/Cheer Athletics/Activities/ to join our program. Apply now.
AVONDALE SCHOOL DISTRICT Join the Avondale Team! At Avondale, we're more than a school districtâwe're a supportive community dedicated to personal growth, resilience, and discovery. We invest in our staff through strong professional development and a collaborative network, empowering educators to create exceptional, individualized learning experiences where every student matters and every story counts . Learn more about us at www.avondaleschools.org . POSITION OPENING: Varsity Sideline and Competitive Cheer Coach 15-20 Hours per Week POSITION: Varsity Sideline and Competitive Cheer Coach REPORTS TO: ATHLETIC DIRECTOR HOURS: 15-20 HOURS PER WEEK JOB GOAL : To provide instruction of high school athletes in the fundamental skills and development of Cheer and also instill moral values, pride of accomplishment, acceptable social behavior, self-discipline and self-confidence WHAT WE NEED: Experience Coaching cheer Knowledge/experience scheduling practices Teach techniques and fundamentals of sport Be able to demonstrate all skills necessary for the sport Be knowledgeable about the rules and regulations of the sport Assume the responsibility for constant care of equipment and facilities being used Conduct structured practices that stay within the confines of the specified times at the Athletic Department Be an example of good character and sportsmanship to team at all times Conduct fundraisers to provide money for team supplies and needs The coach will be responsible for organizing practices, developing skills and routines, promoting sportsmanship, and supporting school spirit while ensuring a safe and positive environment for student-athletes Choreograph and teach cheers, chants, and routines Prepare the team for games, competitions, and school events WHAT YOU NEED: 1. Works cooperatively with administrators, students, parents, and community. 2. Ability to lead, organize and supervise all aspects of the cheer program. 3. The ideal candidate will demonstrate strong leadership, excellent communication skills, and a commitment to developing student-athletes both on and off the mat. 5. Develop a positive team culture focused on sportsmanship, accountability, and academic success. 6. Ensure player safety and adherence to district, league, and MHSAA policies. 7. Coordinate offseason conditioning, strength training, and skill development programs. 8. Maintains control of storage and use of school-owned property. 9. Maintains professional competence. 10. Other duties as assigned by the Athletic Director. WORK LOCATION: Avondale High School 2900 Waukegan St Auburn Hills MI 48326 SALARY DETAILS: Per Master Agreement TERMS OF EMPLOYMENT: Per Master Agreement EVALUATION: Per Master Agreement View Avondale Contract Agreements (Current Bargaining Agreements) WHY WORK FOR AVONDALE? In addition to collaborating with passionate educators on meaningful initiatives, joining Avondale means you'll also enjoy these outstanding benefits:

Job.JobDescription:
Walled Lake Consolidated School District is looking for an Athletics/Activities/ to join our program. Apply now.
Position: Berkley Middle School Robotics Coach Calendar: September 2026 - March 2027 Hours: Approximately 3-5x per week (6-10 hours/week) after school. In season will be September-December (meets 2x/week) plus one weekend competition and out of season will be January through March (meets 1x/week). Stipend : $1500 per season, paid in April 2027 Position Summary: We are seeking a creative, energetic, and knowledgeable Robotics Coach to lead our middle school FIRST Robotics Club offered to 6th-8th Grade students at Anderson and Norup International. This is an after school position ideal for individuals who are passionate about STEM, mentorship, and project-based learning. Whether you're an educator, engineer, college student, or robotics enthusiast, this is a chance to help students develop technical skills, confidence, and teamwork through real-world challenges and competition. Meeting Location and Time : The Den from approximately 4:00-6:00 pm (flexible). Key Responsibilities: Serve as coach and mentor to middle school robotics students Coordinate and facilitate afterschool build sessions and team meetings Guide students through engineering design, programming, project management, and problem-solving Promote student leadership and collaboration Ensure a safe, inclusive, and supportive environment for all students Communicate with students, families, school staff, and FIRST representatives Supervise and support the team at local competitions Manage administrative tasks, including registration, budgeting, and equipment planning Qualifications: Enthusiasm for working with students and a commitment to student growth and empowerment Strong organizational and communication skills Comfort with tools and a general understanding of mechanical and electrical systems Familiarity with programming (Java preferred), CAD or design tools, 3D printing, or CNC (preferred but not required) Experience with FIRST Robotics or similar programs is a plus Ability to lift and move equipment (up to 50 lbs), attend competitions, and supervise students in various settings Must pass all school/district background checks What We Offer: A supportive school community with students excited to learn and build Access to tools, space, and training materials provided by the school and FIRST Opportunities to collaborate with fellow coaches, mentors, and the broader robotics community A chance to inspire the next generation of engineers, problem-solvers, and leaders APPLICATION DEADLINE: Until Filled. Interested candidates should apply online. District Vision Our inclusive culture will inspire, empower and lead all learners to realize their full potential and make a positive difference in their future and community Commitment The Berkley School District is committed to keeping diversity, equity and inclusion at the forefront of its goal of creating an environment where all staff, students and community members feel a sense of belonging. Per School Safety Legislation, each individual, who is either employed full-time or part-time, or is assigned to regularly and continuously work under contract, shall be fingerprinted for the purpose of performing a criminal history background check. Prior to employment, selected candidate must complete the fingerprinting process. Berkley School District does not discriminate on the basis of race, color, national origin, age, sex, sexual orientation, religion, marital status, height, weight or any disability in its educational programs, activities or employment as required by Title VI of the Civil Rights Act of 1964 and Section 504 of the Rehabilitation Act of 1973

Job.JobDescription:
Groves High School is looking for a Volleyball Athletics/Activities/ to join our program. Apply now.
Birmingham Public Schools is seeking a Boys Volleyball Junior Varsity Coach at Groves High School for the 2025-2026 school year. Major Duties and Responsibilities • Assist the head coach in developing and maintaining a program that is positive, safe, educational, and sportsmanlike • Support the planning and implementation of daily practices and training sessions • Assist with preseason workouts, conditioning, and camps as permitted by MHSAA regulations • Provide instruction and skill development to student athletes. • Support supervision of student athletes during practices, competitions, and team activities • Promote positive relationships with student athletes, parents, and community members • Work cooperatively with the head coach, athletic department, and school administration • Assist with team communication and program organization as directed by the head coach • Other duties as assigned by the Athletic Director or Principal Please see the attached posting for additional information. Qualified candidates are invited to send letter of interest and resume to Ross Gauthier, Athletic Director at Groves High School rgauthier@birmingham.k12.mi.us, and include the following information: • Previous Coaching Experience • Experience with sport (participating, coaching, officiating, etc.) • Any other applicable information

Job.JobTitle: Girls Tennis – Junior Varsity Head Coach
Job.JobDescription:
Brandon High School is looking for a Tennis Athletics/Activities/ to join our program. Apply now.
POSITION : Girls Tennis â Junior Varsity Head Coach â req 15842 LOCATION : Brandon High School DESCRIPTION OF THE DISTRICT : The Brandon School District is a mid-size school district nestled in Northwest Oakland County. The district has approximately 1,000 students in junior kindergarten-5th grade between the two elementary schools, approximately 600 at the middle school level and approximately 850 at the high school. This small, tight knit community has a strong connection to its schools and is passionate about the education of its youth. Brandon High School sends 85-90% of its graduates to post-secondary school each year, it was ranked in the 82nd percentile of all schools in the State on the top-to-bottom rankings and it was awarded as a Silver Medal Winner by the US News. Our elementary schools are both one-to-one technology are each near the 83rd percentile in State rankings. MINIMUM QUALIFICATIONS : High School Diploma Working with students in a similar capacity is preferred KNOWLEDGE, SKILLS AND ABILITIES: Must have documented evidence of organizational skills, knowledge, experience, preparation and aptitudes in the above sport commensurate with the level of the position Must have demonstrated ability to work with and motivate young people and adults Must be available during the time requirements of the position Certification in CPR/AED preferred RESPONSIBILITIES: Directly responsible for all matters relative to the program including but not limited to, player personnel, practice sessions, care and maintenance of equipment and supervision of players during practice, games, travel and other team functions. Be knowledgeable of and consistently enforce school, District, League and state association (MHSAA) rules and policies. Effectively communicate with players, officials, fellow coaches, parents, administrators and community members. Ensure that the safety and welfare of the athletes is a top priority in the organization and implementation of practice and game situations. Be responsive to instructions and directions of the district Athletic Director and other appropriate school administrators. Present a professional image and provide a positive role model in terms of personal habits, language and conduct. Other duties as assigned. REPORTS TO : Athletic Director & Varsity Head Coach WORKDAY/WEEK : 20-25 hours per week in season, 10-12 hours per week out of season STARTING DATE : ASAP COMPENSATION : Per the BEA Master Agreement, Athletics Schedule C.2 POSTING DATE: April 8, 2026 POSTING DEADLINE : Internal: Until Filled External: Until Filled The Brandon Board of Education is committed to a policy of non-discrimination in relation to race, color, sex, age, religion, height, weight, gender identity, marital status, disability and national origin. For all concerns and complaints for issues relating to Title IX, Section 504, the Age Discrimination Act and Title II, please contact Megan Koslowski, Title IX Coordinator at 1025 S. Ortonville Road, Ortonville, MI 48462, (248) 627-1800.

Job.JobDescription:
Hazel Park Junior High (High-Needs School) is looking for a Basketball Athletics/Activities/ to join our program. Apply now.
2026-2027 Season 7th Grade Girls Basketball Coach Position Overview: We are seeking dedicated and enthusiastic individuals to join our team as a Junior High School Athletic Coach. As a coach, you will play a pivotal role in shaping the athletic development, character, and success of scholar-athletes. You will be responsible for coaching and mentoring scholar-athletes in various sports, fostering skill development, teamwork, and sportsmanship. This position offers an exciting opportunity to make a positive impact on the lives of young athletes while contributing to the success of our high school sports program. Responsibilities: Coach Scholar-Athletes: Provide instruction, guidance, and mentorship to student-athletes in designated sports, including but not limited to football, basketball, soccer, volleyball, baseball, softball, track and field, wrestling, swimming, tennis, and golf. Plan and Execute Practices: Develop and implement comprehensive practice plans focused on skill development, strategy, and conditioning. Create a positive and engaging practice environment that fosters learning and growth. Strategic Game Planning: Develop game strategies, tactics, and playbooks to maximize team performance during competitions. Make real-time adjustments and decisions during games to optimize team success. Player Development: Assess individual player strengths and weaknesses and provide personalized coaching to enhance skill development and performance. Encourage continuous improvement and set realistic goals for player development. Team Building and Leadership: Foster a positive team culture based on sportsmanship, respect, and teamwork. Promote leadership skills, communication, and collaboration among team members. Academic Emphasis: Instill the importance of academic excellence and balance between athletics and academics among scholar-athletes. Encourage and support scholar-athletes in achieving academic success by monitoring their progress, providing academic resources, and promoting time management and study skills. Collaborate with teachers and administrators to ensure scholar-athletes are meeting academic requirements and maintaining eligibility for participation in sports. Foster a culture of academic achievement and personal growth that prepares scholar-athletes for success both on and off the field. Communication: Maintain open and transparent communication with parents/guardians, school administration, the community and the Athletic department regarding team activities, expectations, and schedules. Build positive relationships with stakeholders to support the success of the sports program. Compliance and Safety: Supervise players during practices, games, travel and other team functions. Ensure compliance with all applicable rules, regulations, and guidelines set forth by the Michigan High School Athletic Association (MHSAA) and the school district. Prioritize the safety and well-being of student-athletes at all times. Be responsive to direction given by the Athletic Director and other appropriate school administrators. Professional Development: Stay informed about current coaching techniques, trends, and best practices in high school sports coaching. Attend coaching clinics, workshops, and training sessions to enhance coaching knowledge and skills. Equipment Management: Assist in tracking and caring for equipment and facilities related to the sport. Qualifications: Previous coaching experience at the high school level or higher preferred. Demonstrated expertise in the designated sport(s), including knowledge of rules, techniques, and strategies. Strong leadership, communication, and interpersonal skills. Ability to motivate, inspire, and mentor scholar-athletes to reach their full potential. Commitment to fostering a positive team culture based on integrity, sportsmanship, and respect. Availability to attend practices, games, and team events during evenings and weekends. Valid Concussion Certification or willingness to obtain certification as required by the MHSAA. Must adhere to the MHSAA policies for coaches. CPR/AED & First Aid Certification required. Must submit to a Finger-Print based Criminal History Record Information (CHRI) check in accordance with the Michigan School Volunteer & Employee Criminal History Program. Note: This job description is intended to outline the general nature and level of work performed by individuals assigned to this position. It is not intended to be an exhaustive list of all responsibilities, duties, and qualifications required of coaches in Michigan high schools. Application Procedure: All Non-Hazel Park Schools Employees will be required to complete an application through EduStaff and will be compensated as a contract employee of EduStaff. Application Deadline: When Filled. All interested applicants need to complete the employment application. Applications will be accepted until the position is filled. The Hazel Park School District does not discriminate on the basis of race, color, national origin, sex, age, disability, height, weight, religion, or marital status in its programs and activities. WE ARE AN EQUAL OPPORTUNITY EMPLOYER
Job.CompanyDescription: Walled Lake Consolidated School District coaching jobs

Demonstrated expertise in the designated sport(s), including knowledge of rules, techniques, and strategies. Strong leadership, communication, and interpersonal skills. Ability to motivate, inspire, and mentor scholar-athletes to reach their full potential. Commitment to fostering a positive team culture based on integrity, sportsmanship, and respect. Availability to attend practices, games, and team events during evenings and weekends. Valid Concussion Certification or willingness to obtain certification as required by the MHSAA. Must adhere to the MHSAA policies for coaches. CPR/AED & First Aid Certification required. Must submit to a Finger-Print based Criminal History Record Information (CHRI) check in accordance with the Michigan School Volunteer & Employee Criminal History Program. Job.JobDescription:
Hazel Park High School (High-Needs School) is looking for a Basketball Athletics/Activities/ to join our program. Apply now.
2026-2027 Season Freshman Boys Basketball Coach Position Overview: We are seeking dedicated and enthusiastic individuals to join our team as a High School Athletic Coach. As a coach, you will play a pivotal role in shaping the athletic development, character, and success of scholar-athletes. You will be responsible for coaching and mentoring scholar-athletes in various sports, fostering skill development, teamwork, and sportsmanship. This position offers an exciting opportunity to make a positive impact on the lives of young athletes while contributing to the success of our high school sports program. Responsibilities: Coach Scholar-Athletes: Provide instruction, guidance, and mentorship to student-athletes in designated sports, including but not limited to football, basketball, soccer, volleyball, baseball, softball, track and field, wrestling, swimming, tennis, and golf. Plan and Execute Practices: Develop and implement comprehensive practice plans focused on skill development, strategy, and conditioning. Create a positive and engaging practice environment that fosters learning and growth. Strategic Game Planning: Develop game strategies, tactics, and playbooks to maximize team performance during competitions. Make real-time adjustments and decisions during games to optimize team success. Player Development: Assess individual player strengths and weaknesses and provide personalized coaching to enhance skill development and performance. Encourage continuous improvement and set realistic goals for player development. Team Building and Leadership: Foster a positive team culture based on sportsmanship, respect, and teamwork. Promote leadership skills, communication, and collaboration among team members. Academic Emphasis: Instill the importance of academic excellence and balance between athletics and academics among scholar-athletes. Encourage and support scholar-athletes in achieving academic success by monitoring their progress, providing academic resources, and promoting time management and study skills. Collaborate with teachers and administrators to ensure scholar-athletes are meeting academic requirements and maintaining eligibility for participation in sports. Foster a culture of academic achievement and personal growth that prepares scholar-athletes for success both on and off the field. Communication: Maintain open and transparent communication with parents/guardians, school administration, the community and the Athletic department regarding team activities, expectations, and schedules. Build positive relationships with stakeholders to support the success of the sports program. Compliance and Safety: Supervise players during practices, games, travel and other team functions. Ensure compliance with all applicable rules, regulations, and guidelines set forth by the Michigan High School Athletic Association (MHSAA) and the school district. Prioritize the safety and well-being of student-athletes at all times. Be responsive to direction given by the Athletic Director and other appropriate school administrators. Professional Development: Stay informed about current coaching techniques, trends, and best practices in high school sports coaching. Attend coaching clinics, workshops, and training sessions to enhance coaching knowledge and skills. Equipment Management: Assist in tracking and caring for equipment and facilities related to the sport. Qualifications: Previous coaching experience at the high school level or higher preferred. Demonstrated expertise in the designated sport(s), including knowledge of rules, techniques, and strategies. Strong leadership, communication, and interpersonal skills. Ability to motivate, inspire, and mentor scholar-athletes to reach their full potential. Commitment to fostering a positive team culture based on integrity, sportsmanship, and respect. Availability to attend practices, games, and team events during evenings and weekends. Valid Concussion Certification or willingness to obtain certification as required by the MHSAA. Must adhere to the MHSAA policies for coaches. CPR/AED & First Aid Certification required. Must submit to a Finger-Print based Criminal History Record Information (CHRI) check in accordance with the Michigan School Volunteer & Employee Criminal History Program. Note: This job description is intended to outline the general nature and level of work performed by individuals assigned to this position. It is not intended to be an exhaustive list of all responsibilities, duties, and qualifications required of coaches in Michigan high schools. Responsibilities: Coach Scholar-Athletes: Provide instruction, guidance, and mentorship to student-athletes in designated sports, including but not limited to football, basketball, soccer, volleyball, baseball, softball, track and field, wrestling, swimming, tennis, and golf. Plan and Execute Practices: Develop and implement comprehensive practice plans focused on skill development, strategy, and conditioning. Create a positive and engaging practice environment that fosters learning and growth. Strategic Game Planning: Develop game strategies, tactics, and playbooks to maximize team performance during competitions. Make real-time adjustments and decisions during games to optimize team success. Player Development: Assess individual player strengths and weaknesses and provide personalized coaching to enhance skill development and performance. Encourage continuous improvement and set realistic goals for player development. Team Building and Leadership: Foster a positive team culture based on sportsmanship, respect, and teamwork. Promote leadership skills, communication, and collaboration among team members. Academic Emphasis: Instill the importance of academic excellence and balance between athletics and academics among scholar-athletes. Encourage and support scholar-athletes in achieving academic success by monitoring their progress, providing academic resources, and promoting time management and study skills. Collaborate with teachers and administrators to ensure scholar-athletes are meeting academic requirements and maintaining eligibility for participation in sports. Foster a culture of academic achievement and personal growth that prepares scholar-athletes for success both on and off the field. Communication: Maintain open and transparent communication with parents/guardians, school administration, the community and the Athletic department regarding team activities, expectations, and schedules. Build positive relationships with stakeholders to support the success of the sports program. Compliance and Safety: Supervise players during practices, games, travel and other team functions. Ensure compliance with all applicable rules, regulations, and guidelines set forth by the Michigan High School Athletic Association (MHSAA) and the school district. Prioritize the safety and well-being of student-athletes at all times. Be responsive to direction given by the Athletic Director and other appropriate school administrators. Professional Development: Stay informed about current coaching techniques, trends, and best practices in high school sports coaching. Attend coaching clinics, workshops, and training sessions to enhance coaching knowledge and skills. Equipment Management: Assist in tracking and caring for equipment and facilities related to the sport. Qualifications: Previous coaching experience at the high school level or higher preferred. Demonstrated expertise in the designated sport(s), including knowledge of rules, techniques, and strategies. Strong leadership, communication, and interpersonal skills. Ability to motivate, inspire, and mentor scholar-athletes to reach their full potential. Commitment to fostering a positive team culture based on integrity, sportsmanship, and respect. Availability to attend practices, games, and team events during evenings and weekends. Valid Concussion Certification or willingness to obtain certification as required by the MHSAA. Must adhere to the MHSAA policies for coaches. CPR/AED & First Aid Certification required. Must submit to a Finger-Print based Criminal History Record Information (CHRI) check in accordance with the Michigan School Volunteer & Employee Criminal History Program. Note: This job description is intended to outline the general nature and level of work performed by individuals assigned to this position. It is not intended to be an exhaustive list of all responsibilities, duties, and qualifications required of coaches in Michigan high schools. Application Procedure: All Non-Hazel Park Schools Employees will be required to complete an application through EduStaff and will be compensated as a contract employee of EduStaff. Application Deadline: When Filled. All interested applicants need to complete the employment application. Applications will be accepted until the position is filled. The Hazel Park School District does not discriminate on the basis of race, color, national origin, sex, age, disability, height, weight, religion, or marital status in its programs and activities. WE ARE AN EQUAL OPPORTUNITY EMPLOYER
